intent: Add python_pkg/byox_ladder: a generator that ranks the build-your-own-x projects by difficulty and produces two offline HTML ladders (per-guide and per-category), committed as runnable pages.
scope: python_pkg/byox_ladder/ (new package: parse_guides.py, build_pages.py, templates, built HTML, tests)
docs/superpowers/contracts + evidence artifacts for this change
No changes to existing packages, tooling, or the unrelated working-tree edits (i3 config, feedback-appimage)
changes: parse_guides.py parses the upstream README into guides.json; a guide inherits its category tier, language only nudges within-tier order
build_pages.py inlines guides.json into the guide template and wraps both pages in a standalone HTML skeleton
Makefile drives fetch -> parse -> build; byox-readme.md and guides.json are gitignored derived artifacts
tests/ cover both modules to 100% branch coverage

verification.evidence: 21 passed; coverage 100.00% (parse_guides.py 69 stmts/16 branches, build_pages.py 32 stmts/4 branches, __init__ 0) with 0 missing
ruff, ruff-format, mypy, pylint, bandit, codespell, check-python-location, and pytest-coverage all reported Passed
wrote guides.json (359 guides); wrote guide-ladder.html and category-ladder.html, both starting with <!doctype html>

risks: Upstream README format drift could break the regex parser; parse_guides.py exits non-zero and the committed HTML stays valid until regenerated
Difficulty tiers are a subjective judgment; within-tier order is approximate by design
rollback: git rm -r python_pkg/byox_ladder and delete the two docs/superpowers artifacts, then commit
After rollback, run pre-commit run --all-files to confirm the tree is still green
